verdict_reasoning: FOLR1 encodes folate receptor alpha, a GPI-anchored cell surface receptor. The NCBI summary explicitly states the protein 'anchors to membranes via a glycosyl-phosphatidylinositol linkage.' GPI anchors stably tether proteins to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane via their own lipid-attachment mechanism, making the protein body accessible from the extracellular face. Soluble isoforms may also exist but do not alter the surface accessibility of the anchored form.
